_comment: Per-chart narratives. CI (build_prompt.py -> GitHub Models -> write_narrative.py) overwrites these from live numbers; this committed copy is the fallback so the page always reads well. source = 'fallback' | 'github-models'.

notes.basket: The breakfast basket, an average index of coffee, sugar, wheat, and orange juice prices set to 100 in 2015, stands at 132, indicating a 32% increase since that baseline year. This rise reflects an uneven and relatively recent climb in global soft commodity prices. The index aggregates individual commodity price changes to offer a broad perspective on cost trends for common breakfast items. Variability among components means some have surged far more than others, influencing the overall basket level.
notes.movers: Coffee prices have increased sharply by 93% since 2015, making it the largest contributor to the basket's rise. Orange juice follows with a 22% increase, while wheat and sugar have seen more modest gains of 7% and 5% respectively. These shifts largely stem from weather events and crop diseases—such as citrus greening and hurricanes affecting orange juice, and frost and drought impacting arabica coffee production—rather than broad-based inflationary pressures. Soft commodity markets are relatively thin, which amplifies price volatility when supply is disrupted.
notes.why: The headline increase in this breakfast commodity basket is driven primarily by a few weather-sensitive and disease-exposed crops like coffee and orange juice. Because their supply conditions are vulnerable, prices can remain elevated over multiple years following adverse events. This underlines the importance of understanding weather and biological factors in evaluating soft commodity price trends.
